Customers can register their preferences on the DND registry for blocking.

items.summary: Transport operators are avoiding Manipur due to high costs and long travel times. Essential goods face shortages as highway conditions worsen significantly. Freight charges have increased substantially, impacting commodity prices. Retail prices for onions, potatoes, and edible oil have risen sharply. Further disruptions risk shortages, especially during the monsoon season.

items.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a substantial rise of $7.26 billion. These reserves reached $674.19 billion in the week ending July 326, 2026. The Reserve Bank of India closely monitors foreign exchange market developments. The RBI intervenes when necessary to ensure orderly market conditions. The central bank refrains from targeting any specific exchange rate.

items.title: Expert view: Recent correction has provided ample opportunities in large caps, says DSP MF's market strategist
items.summary: In a recent interview, Sahil Kapoor of DSP Mutual Fund shares insights on leveraging current market corrections to invest in large-cap equities. Kapoor highlights sectoral opportunities like private banks and healthcare. &nbsp;

items.title: Mumbai Metro One restructures debt with NARCL, avoids insolvency
items.summary: Mumbai Metro One Private Limited has successfully restructured its debt, avoiding insolvency. The company signed a Master Restructuring Agreement with NARCL for Rs 2,771.32 crore. This agreement strengthens the financial position of the metro operator. NARCL will now nominate a director to the company's board. A monitoring committee will oversee the restructuring implementation process.

items.title: Nykaa among 6 midcap stocks that hit 52-week highs, rally up to 30% in a month
items.summary: Nykaa, Godrej Industries and four other BSE MidCap stocks hit fresh 52-week highs, rallying up to 30% over the past month amid strong market momentum.

items.title: Expert view: Indian stock market may remain range-bound this year
items.summary: As foreign portfolio investors sell, domestic institutional investors rally, keeping the Indian stock market stable. Nikhil Chawla, managing partner and co-founder of xMultiplied Capital Advisors, however, warns that domestic support cannot fully replace foreign capital.&nbsp;

items.title: Aastha Spintex to acquire Falcon Yarns to expand manufacturing capacity
items.summary: Aastha Spintex has acquired Falcon Yarns to significantly expand its manufacturing capacity.  This strategic acquisition will increase the company's spinning capacity to over seventeen thousand metric tons.  The combined entity will possess a spindle capacity exceeding sixty-one thousand spindles.  This expansion aims to meet rising customer demand and improve operational efficiencies.  The deal strengthens Aastha Spintex's position in the cotton yarn industry.

items.title: Shapoorji Pallonji prices Rs 21, 500 cr financing with Deutsche, BlackRock among investors
items.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group secured significant funding through debt issuance.  This refinancing effort attracted both domestic and global institutional investors.  The company raised approximately Rs 21,500 crore in its initial funding tranche.  Investors showed strong interest, with many participating for the first time.  This move is part of a larger plan to replace higher cost debt.

items.title: Aukera raises ₹90 crore debt funding to expand lab-grown diamond jewellery business
items.summary: In a significant move, lab-grown diamond brand Aukera raised ₹90 crore in debt financing, spearheaded by Alteria Capital. This influx of capital is earmarked for enhancing retail presence and advancing product innovation. Aukera's strategy includes opening additional outlets in both familiar and new cities, as the company targets the upscale segment of the booming market, ensuring a robust balance sheet for upcoming industry shifts.

items.title: India's EV components opportunity to hit Rs 3.55 lakh crore by 2032 amid localisation push
items.summary: India's electric vehicle components market is projected to grow significantly. This market is expected to reach approximately Rs 3.55 lakh crore by 2032. Battery packs will dominate over half of this component market. However, battery packs and inverters remain import-dependent segments. Strategic investments will secure India's global EV value chain position.

items.title: IndiGo gets DGCA warning over cargo spillage, dangerous goods rule violations
items.summary: IndiGo's parent company received a DGCA warning letter for procedural deviations. This followed an audit after a cargo spillage incident occurred in January. The airline must now submit an action taken report to the aviation regulator. No penalties or sanctions were imposed by the DGCA on the airline. The company cited internal communication gaps for a delayed disclosure of this event.

items.title: Global PE firm Invus nears  ₹500 crore minority stake in restaurant operator Popo Ventures
items.summary: Invus Group is close to investing  ₹500 crore in Bengaluru-based restaurant operator Popo Ventures, underscoring rising private equity interest in India's fast-growing dining sector.

This offering is expected to be India's largest dairy sector IPO.  Proceeds will fund manufacturing expansion and debt repayment.  The company has completed investor roadshows for the upcoming launch.

items.title: Is it time to exit AI stocks? Here's what Jefferies' GREED & Fear report said
items.summary: Brokerage Jefferies has cautioned that investors are showing signs of "AI fatigue" after the sharp rally in artificial intelligence-linked stocks, leading to expectations of a shift towards cheaper value stocks. However, in its latest GREED & Fear report, the brokerage said the long-term AI investment cycle remains intact, backed by strong spending on AI infrastructure and continued demand for computing power.

items.title: EPF Withdrawal Guide: Know eligibility, rules and conditions to withdraw full or partial PF
items.summary: What is UAN: A UAN or Universal Account Number is a 12-digit number given to EPFO members. It is a lifetime identity and can be used while you switch jobs and get a new member ID in that particular company. Your ID can be linked to UAN. This helps keep all your PF contributions centralised.

items.title: RBI is right to act on digital payment fraud. But some safeguards need sharper design
items.summary: Cyber fraud complaints and losses are increasing significantly in India's digital payment system. The Reserve Bank of India has proposed new safeguards to address these growing concerns. A one-hour lag on large transactions aims to reduce financial losses from fraud. Additional authentication for vulnerable users and scrutiny of mule accounts are also suggested. Customer-induced controls and a kill switch offer users more transaction management capabilities.

items.title: Swiggy resolves FSSAI licence issue linked to subsidiary Toing; says no food safety risk
items.summary: Swiggy said it has resolved an FSSAI licence-related issue concerning its food ordering and delivery platform Toing after updating licence particulars. items.title: The return to flying faster than sound will start small
items.summary: The FAA's proposal to lift the decades-old ban on supersonic flights over land could revive civilian faster-than-sound travel. Powered by quieter boom technology demonstrated by NASA's X-59, the first commercial opportunity is expected to be high-end private jets, with airliners remaining a longer-term prospect due to cost, technology and regulatory hurdles.

The Japanese yen gained momentum due to pension funds' activities, and heightened hedging by importers swayed the rupee's demand among merchants.

Non-compliant colleges must implement requirements without delay and seek assistance if needed.

The diversified business group operates across energy, IVD, and MedTech sectors. The listing reflects a commitment to transparency and sustainable growth for stakeholders. The company aims to enhance market presence and create value across its verticals.

Thirty long-distance and inter-city trains have been cancelled by Central Railway. Repair work is ongoing, but challenging conditions persist for operations. Passengers should check official channels for updated train service information.

items.title: YES Bank: Positive rating upgrades reflect sustained strengthening of credit profile
items.summary: Credit rating agencies such as S&P, Moody’s, ICRA and CareEdge have affirmed or upgraded the lender’s ratings, citing stronger fundamentals and a stable outlook.

items.title: Equity MF inflow surges 26% to ₹28,973-cr in June

items.title: India extends anti-dumping duty on seamless tubes, pipes till Jan 2027
items.summary: The decision aims to protect local manufacturers from the adverse effects of low-priced imports, with the duty now set to remain in place for an additional 3 years

items.title: Global Market: European shares edge higher as miners, airlines offset tech weakness; benchmark set for weekly loss
items.summary: European shares saw minimal change as mining and travel stocks gained. Technology shares declined, weighing on the broader market's performance. Investors remained cautious due to renewed Middle East geopolitical tensions. EasyJet shares surged significantly after a takeover approach was revealed.

items.title: DTC says no free bus service for women in Delhi without Pink Saheli Card
items.summary: Delhi Transport Corporation will transition to a smart card system for free women's bus travel. Existing paper pink tickets will be valid for free travel until July thirty-first. After August first, only smart card holders can receive pink tickets for free rides. Women without the Pink Saheli Smart Card must purchase regular tickets for their journeys. Passengers are advised to obtain their smart cards before the July deadline.

items.title: SBI sells 1.42% stake for ₹1,655 cr in SBI Funds Management ahead of arm's IPO
items.summary: SBI sold the shares to investors including 360 ONE funds, Tata AIG General Insurance Company, Go Digit General Insurance, Bennett Coleman, Anand Rathi Global Finance, Capri Global Ventures and Carnelian Bharat Amritkaal Fund

items.title: Wheels India board approves fundraising plans of up to ₹400 crore
items.summary: The funds are to be raised by way of issuance of equity shares of face value ₹10 and/or equity linked instruments in one or more tranches

items.title: Gillette India reschedules board meeting date to declare dividend. Record date, other details
items.summary: Gillette India has moved its AGM to 24 August 2026, with dividends payable by 30 September 2026 if approved. The company reported a 21.3% rise in net profit to  ₹192.51 crore for Q1 FY26, alongside a 3.2% revenue increase.

items.title: Global Market: IEA sees global oil demand recovering as Strait of Hormuz flows resume
items.summary: Global oil demand is showing signs of recovery as shipments through the Strait of Hormuz resume and crude prices ease, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said. While the agency raised its demand outlook slightly, it cautioned that consumption and production remain well below pre-conflict levels amid lingering Middle East tensions.

items.title: Mint Explainer: What is the CCI complaint against Flipkart over its marketplace discounting model?
items.summary: A new CCI complaint alleges Flipkart runs a ‘recurring subsidy pool’, funnelling tax and operational gains from group entities to just 33 preferred sellers who then undercut independent sellers on price.&nbsp;

items.title: Power giant NTPC seeks uranium assets overseas in nuclear push
items.summary: India's largest power producer seeks overseas uranium mines for future nuclear capacity. NTPC plans to build thirty gigawatts of nuclear power over two decades. This move supports India's broader push to decarbonize its economy. The company is looking at assets in Canada, Australia, and Kazakhstan. This strategy addresses domestic fuel limitations and growing energy needs.

items.title: India's middle class may have little room in the richer housing market
items.summary: India's residential property market delivered one of its strongest first-half performances in over a decade, with home sales staying near record highs even as growth stabilised. Knight Frank India says premium housing is now driving the market, while affordable homes continue to shrink. Mumbai led sales, Bengaluru posted the fastest growth and NCR remained the weakest market.

items.title: IPO GMP buzz: Kusumgar leads with 39% GMP; SBI Funds Management, Laser Power also attract strong interest
items.summary: The IPO market shows strong investor interest with high grey market premiums. Kusumgar IPO leads with a nearly 39% estimated listing gain. SBI Funds Management IPO also attracts significant attention with a 16% expected upside. Laser Power & Infra IPO offers a potential 15-16% listing gain. Alpine Texworld IPO shows muted interest with an estimated gain of 2%.

items.title: Govt tightens sale of liquid medicines with high alcohol content
items.summary: With their inclusion under Schedule H1, these medicines can now be sold only against the prescription of a registered medical practitioner and supplied only through regulated pharmaceutical distribution chain

items.title: Is energy efficiency a communist conspiracy? Trump’s administration seems to think so
items.summary: Despite a history of energy-conservation measures saving billions of dollars for America, US President Donald Trump and his party seem to be doing everything in their power to sabotage such efforts. Calling its proponents ‘communists’ is just the tip of the iceberg.

items.title: Private banks to outperform PSU banks in earnings growth during FY26-28: MOFS
items.summary: The report predicts credit growth in the mid-to-high teens for FY27, with private sector banks expected to outperform public sector banks

items.title: E20 petrol may cut mileage by 3-5% in some vehicles, but benefits outweigh impact: Oil Ministry
items.summary: The government acknowledges E20 fuel's minor fuel economy reduction. This impact is outweighed by benefits like better engine performance and lower emissions. India's ethanol blending program began with pilot projects in 2001. The nation achieved ten percent blending ahead of schedule in 2022.

items.title: India sweetens the deal for global electronics manufacturers
items.summary: India is expanding electronics manufacturing by offering customs duty concessions on machinery. These incentives aim to attract investment in critical components and advanced equipment. The government seeks to deepen the domestic electronics ecosystem beyond final product assembly. This policy supports the growth of India's electronics production and export capabilities. New Delhi is positioning India as a comprehensive electronics manufacturing hub.

items.title: Global Market: Geopolitical risks return to centre stage for ECB as inflation fears resurface
items.summary: Fresh US-Iran hostilities have pushed the European Central Bank back to its inflation fight. Rising energy prices now threaten to reignite price pressures across the euro zone. This renewed conflict has heightened uncertainty around the inflation outlook for policymakers. Market expectations have shifted, with traders betting on further ECB rate hikes. Developments in the Middle East are now a key factor for future monetary policy.

items.title: Fresh US-Iran clash clouds IEA oil outlook despite June supply lift from Hormuz reopening
items.summary: Global oil supply rebounded in June after a ceasefire allowed Strait of Hormuz transit. However, renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran threaten future oil surplus forecasts. The International Energy Agency sees potential disruption to its 2027 surplus projection. Refined product markets remain tight due to refinery issues and Ukrainian attacks. Global oil demand is recovering, but overall supply forecasts depend on lasting peace.

items.title: Small-cap industrial stock Interarch Building Products extends gains after this business update; details here
items.summary: In an exchange filing during market hours on 9 July, Interarch Building Solutions said it inaugurated its new manufacturing facility at Kheda, Gujarat, strengthening its footprint across India.

items.title: Newgen Software shares surge 15% as broad-based IT stocks rally
items.summary: Newgen Software shares experienced a significant surge, mirroring a broader IT sector advance. The company announced key leadership changes, appointing Tarun Nandwani as CEO. Investors await the Q1 FY27 results on July 16 for business outlook. The stock shows improving technical momentum with a neutral RSI reading. Institutional ownership saw a reduction in the March 2026 quarter.

items.title: Honda to launch 2-3 premium motorcycles, new electric scooters in India this year
items.summary: Honda Motorcycle & Scooter India plans to launch new premium motorcycles this year. The company will also introduce new electric scooter models to the market. This move targets India's growing premium motorcycle segment and rising incomes. Honda is expanding manufacturing capacity and localising components for new models.

items.title: Silver futures decline to ₹2.24 lakh/kg
items.summary: On MCX, silver contracts for September delivery declined by ₹1,934, or 0.85 per cent, to ₹2,24,443 per kg in a business turnover of 2,345 lots.

items.title: Why TCS employee addition just hit 4-year high amid AI job loss fears
items.summary: TCS added 9,279 employees in Q1FY27, marking its strongest quarterly hiring in four years amid growing concerns over AI-led job cuts. The IT major said AI will reshape roles rather than eliminate jobs, while investments in talent, AI services and digital capabilities signal confidence in future demand and growth.

items.title: India lays out tariffs and quotas for U.K. vehicles under trade deal
items.summary: As per the Directorate General of Foreign Trade’s notification, a total of 20,000 completely built units (CBUs) of petrol and diesel passenger vehicles will be allowed for import from the U.K. at concessional rates of 30-50%, depending on the size of the car, down from the normal import duty of between 66-110%

items.title: India extends anti-dumping duty on seamless tubes, pipes till Jan 2027
items.summary: India has extended anti-dumping duties on certain Chinese tubes and pipes. This measure will protect domestic manufacturers from cheap inbound shipments until January 2027. The government also continues anti-dumping duties on Normal Butanol imports. These duties apply to products exported from Malaysia, South Africa, and the United States. Anti-dumping measures ensure fair trade and a level playing field for domestic industries.

items.title: Natural gas companies are set to lose steam in Q1
items.summary: Lower LNG imports, higher gas procurement costs and supply disruptions triggered by the West Asia war are expected to weigh on Q1FY27 earnings of GAIL, Petronet LNG and city gas distributors.

items.summary: Jio Financial Services will announce its Q1FY27 earnings on July 16, 2026. Following the board meeting, an analyst presentation will be hosted via JioEvents. The stock has seen fluctuations, gaining 5% over a month, yet declining by 27% over the past year.

items.title: Adani Power, Adani Green, Adani Ports to Adani Enterprises: Gautam Adani group stocks rise up to 5%. Do you own any?

items.title: SBI MF IPO: Can Zerodha, Groww and other digital-first funds threaten India’s largest AMC in $1 trillion race?
items.summary: As SBI Funds Management heads for its Rs 11,693-crore IPO, the rise of digital-first platforms like Zerodha and Groww is reshaping India's mutual fund industry. While digital challengers are winning younger investors, the country's largest AMC believes its scale, trust and distribution network remain powerful competitive advantages.

items.title: ITR Filing Deadline 2026: July 31, August 31 or October 31? Know which date applies to you
items.summary: ITR Filing Deadline 2026: In a conversation on Zee Business, tax experts Sunil Garg and Vimal Jain explained key changes taxpayers need to know before filing their returns for Assessment Year (AY) 2026-27 and which ITR filing deadline applies to which taxpayers.

items.title: Exclusive: Anand Rathi Wealth to enter mutual fund space with Fund of Funds; launch targeted in 6-9 months
items.summary: Speaking to Zee Business Managing Editor Anil Singhvi after the company's June quarter earnings, Rathi said the reported decline in margins was not reflective of the underlying business performance.

items.title: Bitcoin holds above $63,000 despite ETF outflows; $1.4 billion options expiry in focus
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading above the $63,000 mark on Friday.  The cryptocurrency saw a 2.39% increase in the past twenty-four hours.  Markets are now focused on Friday's $1.4 billion options expiry event.  Global crypto market capitalization rose by 2.02% to $2.19 trillion.  Experts advise caution until Bitcoin clears $67,000 with stronger ETF demand.

items.title: Eternal vs Swiggy Q1FY27 Preview: Which quick commerce firm is likely to lead in June quarter?
items.summary: The spotlight will be on food delivery and quick commerce companies, Eternal and Swiggy, as they prepare to announce their June quarter (Q1FY27) results. According to Zee Business estimates, both companies are expected to post healthy revenue growth during the quarter

items.title: Indian Bank Q1 Results: Standalone net profit rises 10% YoY to Rs 3,273 crore; shares zoom 10%
items.summary: Indian Bank reported a standalone net profit of Rs 3,273 crore for Q1 FY27, up more than 10% year-on-year, while net interest income rose nearly 17%. The PSU lender also posted improved asset quality and higher advances, prompting its shares to surge around 10% after the earnings announcement.

items.title: NSE crude oil options hit record Rs 2,006 crore premium turnover in one day, volumes top 47 lakh
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) reported record activity in its crude oil options contract on July 9, 2026, with the highest-ever daily premium turnover of Rs 2,006.49 crore, trading volume of 47,33,862 contracts, and intraday open interest exceeding 1,14,000 contracts. The milestone reflects growing participation as traders and hedgers increasingly use the contract to manage price risks in a volatile energy market.

items.title: Gold futures drop to ₹1,44,670/10g
items.summary: On the Multi Commodity Exchange, August gold contracts fell by Rs 630, or 0.43%, to Rs 1,44,670 per 10 grams, with a turnover of 975 lots

items.title: Why Costco stock tumbled over 4% despite strong June sales
items.summary: Costco reported strong June sales growth of 10.6% year over year.  This growth, however, marked a slowdown from the previous month's pace.  Investors reacted negatively to signs of moderating sales momentum.  The company's premium valuation amplified the market's sharp reaction to the news.

items.title: Global Market | Tencent explores stake in AI Startup Manus after China challenges Meta deal: Reports
items.summary: Tencent is in talks to become the largest shareholder in AI startup Manus as investors seek to buy back the company from Meta after Chinese regulators ordered the US tech giant to unwind its $2 billion acquisition. The proposed deal would reshape Manus' ownership amid heightened regulatory scrutiny of cross-border AI investments.

items.title: Meta to put AI chip into production in September as it looks to double computing capacity, memo shows
items.summary: To expand computing infrastructure, Meta has secured long-term, multi-year supply agreements, the memo showed

items.title: ITR Filing 2026: Income Tax Department shares tips to follow before filing your ITR
items.summary: The I-T department suggests you choose the appropriate tax regime based on your income and tax liability. There are two tax regimes- the old tax regime and the new tax regime. You can choose any of them. The government introduced the new tax regime in the Union Budget of February 2020.

items.title: Bharti Airtel fixes record date for its highest-ever dividend of Rs 24/share. What’s the last date to buy?
items.summary: Bharti Airtel has fixed July 24 as the record date for its highest-ever annual dividend of Rs 24 per share for FY26, making July 23 the last date to buy the stock for eligibility. The announcement comes as brokerages remain bullish on the telecom major's long-term growth and cash flow prospects.

items.title: EasyJet accepts rival takeover bid by US investor Apollo
items.summary: US private equity firm Apollo has made a higher offer for EasyJet. This new bid surpasses Castlelake's previous proposal for the British airline. EasyJet's board now favors Apollo's offer, signaling a potential bidding war. Apollo must finalize its offer by August 7 under UK regulations. The airline reported increased losses earlier this year due to soaring fuel prices.

items.title: Global Market: GIC reshuffles senior investment leadership, names two deputy group CIOs
items.summary: Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC has appointed two deputy group chief investment officers and named a new chief investment officer for its Fixed Income & Multi Asset business as part of a senior leadership reshuffle. The changes aim to strengthen oversight of the fund's global investment operations.

items.title: US Stock Market: Private credit lending slumps in Q2 despite rebound in fundraising
items.summary: US private credit funds raised significant capital in the second quarter. Direct lending activity, however, saw a sharp decline during the same period. This divergence highlights investor capital outpacing available new lending opportunities. Private equity-backed lending experienced the most substantial decrease in deal volume. Investors now prioritize underwriting discipline and risk-adjusted returns over deployment speed.

items.title: RBI rate hike: Interest burden for Indians may go up later this year amid inflation risks
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is poised to keep its policy rates steady in the short term. Nonetheless, BofA Securities predicts that rising concerns over domestic inflation could lead to increased rates in the future. Revisions to FY27 economic growth projections indicate a stronger demand trend, while external accounts are anticipated to show improvement with a reduced current account deficit.

items.title: E20 costs more at current crude prices, but saves forex, boosts farmers' income, reduce oil dependence: Govt
items.summary: Ethanol blending shields Indian consumers from volatile global oil prices. This program reduces dependence on imported crude oil significantly. It also strengthens the country's long-term energy security and foreign exchange reserves. The initiative has already saved over one lakh ninety-seven thousand crore rupees. Continued blending promises greater fuel price stability and farmer income.

items.title: Critical mineral push calls for board-level focus on communication: Experts
items.summary: India's focus on critical mineral self-reliance is shifting mining governance priorities. Companies now need stakeholder confidence and social license for project success. Boards must integrate communication and stakeholder intelligence into strategic decisions. This approach strengthens governance and improves the execution of strategic initiatives. Transparent communication builds public trust and reinforces investor confidence in the industry.

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers India share price jumps over 36% in three days. Buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: The rally in Kalyan Jewellers share price today was supported by robust volumes, as around 8 crore equity shares changed hands on the stock exchanges on July 10, significantly higher than one week average trading volumes of 4 crore shares and one month average volumes of 2 crore shares.

items.title: Dr Reddy's shares crash 9% in 2 days; brokerages slash target prices after semaglutide supply disruption. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories shares extended their decline after the company delayed commercial supplies of its semaglutide product due to a quality-related API issue. While brokerages remain broadly positive on the long-term outlook, several have cut target prices and earnings estimates, citing near-term execution risks and slower product ramp-up.

items.title: SHANTI Act: The law ....may've started to show results
items.summary: The SHANTI Act 2025 explained: India's nuclear power expansion is gaining momentum through new legislation. The SHANTI Act 2025 allows private participation across the civilian nuclear sector. This law replaces older acts and modernizes the nuclear framework for future growth. Australia's uranium exports will also support India's expanding nuclear generation capacity. Manufacturers are preparing to scale up equipment production for rising demand.

items.title: Ashok Leyland, Hero Motocorp to M&M: Auto stocks rise despite E20 row
items.summary: The Nifty Auto index gained 1% amid E20 fuel controversy, supported by broader market gains. Auto stocks varied in performance; some surged while others, like Bosch, faced losses. Union ministers defended E20, emphasizing its benefits despite criticism from vehicle owners.

items.title: Vedanta Power, Vedanta Oil & Gas, other Vedanta stocks surge up to 6%. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Vedanta Group stocks experienced significant gains on Friday after their recent demerger. Vedanta Iron and Steel shares surged five percent, hitting the upper circuit limit. Vedanta Oil and Gas shares also saw a substantial increase, extending recent positive momentum. Vedanta Power and Vedanta Aluminium reported production and sales figures for the first quarter. Analysts at Motilal Oswal initiated coverage on Vedanta Aluminium with a buy rating.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty hold gains at midday; financials, Adani stocks take over from IT as breadth stays strong

items.title: Bharti Airtel declares record date for the payment of final dividend in FY26. Details here
items.summary: Bharti Airtel has set July 24, 2026, as the record date to determine shareholders for its final dividend for FY26, pending approval at the AGM on August 3, 2026. The proposed dividend is  ₹24 per fully paid equity share and  ₹6 for partly paid shares.

items.title: World Bank Group commits $890 million to promote solar rooftop project in India
items.summary: The World Bank committed USD 890 million for India's solar rooftop initiative. This funding aims to bring clean energy to millions of homes nationwide. The program will also create significant job opportunities across the renewable energy sector. India seeks to achieve net zero emissions by 2070 and increase non-fossil fuels. This support will help scale residential solar and reduce household electricity costs.

items.title: Medicines with over 12% alcohol can no longer be sold without prescription: Govt
items.summary: In a new regulatory change, medications with alcohol content exceeding twelve percent will require a prescription from a doctor. This decision treats these products like standard prescription drugs, compelling manufacturers to obtain the necessary licenses. Furthermore, pharmacies are now tasked with rigorous record-keeping to mitigate potential abuse and ensure that genuine medical use is preserved under enhanced scrutiny.

items.title: Global Market: Sovereign wealth funds pivot to strategic investments as AI takes centre stage
items.summary: Sovereign wealth funds are increasingly prioritising national strategic goals over pure financial returns, with artificial intelligence and semiconductors emerging as key investment areas. Backed by governments, these funds are making fewer but significantly larger investments to strengthen technological leadership, supply chains and long-term economic resilience.

items.summary: Cupid shares extended their rally after the company reaffirmed strong growth momentum, guided for Q1FY27 revenue of over Rs 150 crore and raised its full-year revenue outlook. items.summary: Alpine Texworld IPO price band is  ₹100-105, with subscriptions from July 14-16. The firm aims to raise funds for expansion, including investments in a weaving unit and debt repayment, boosting its financial stability in the textile sector.

items.title: Which countries use E20 or higher ethanol blends? A global guide
items.summary: Countries using E20 fuel: India has achieved its nationwide E20 ethanol blending target ahead of schedule. This transition significantly reduces import dependence and strengthens national energy security. Domestic ethanol production capacity has expanded substantially to meet fuel demand. While new vehicles are E20 compatible, older ones remain on the road. India now joins global leaders like Brazil in adopting renewable fuel standards.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why India is launching an index of services production
items.summary: The index of services production is intended to complement the index of industrial production by providing high-frequency information on the services economy, enabling better policy decisions and economic forecasting.

items.title: India's diamond export pain is hiding a brighter story at home
items.summary: India's diamond exports have declined over recent financial years. Retail giants are increasing investments in both natural and lab-grown diamonds. This domestic focus targets a growing demand from younger Indian consumers. Retailers are expanding offerings to cater to evolving preferences for lightweight jewelry. Natural diamonds continue to hold a significant position in premium portfolios.

items.title: HFCL shares rally 4% after Rs 495 crore order win. Should you buy after 200% surge in 2026?
items.summary: HFCL shares witnessed an uptick on Friday after the company secured a Rs 496 crore overseas order for data centre connectivity solutions, extending their 220% rally in 2026. items.summary: SBI Funds Management IPO: Eligible SBI shareholders must have a valid Permanent Account Number (PAN) that is updated in SBI’s shareholder records. They must also hold a valid demat account, as IPO shares can be allotted only in dematerialised form.

items.title: TCS’ sparse Q1 positives won't unlock re-rating triggers
items.summary: From its 52-week high of  ₹3,425 seen on 9 July last year, the TCS stock is down 40% as meaningful growth triggers remain elusive.

Premium Economy will also become available on six Indian destinations by month's end. The airline cites strong demand as a reason for these network enhancements. Emirates is also opening new retail travel stores and a call centre in India.

He reiterated that policymakers will remain guided by incoming economic data amid persistent inflation uncertainty.

items.title: E20 Petrol: Why India wants ethanol blending and what are your worries? Key questions answered
items.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum released FAQs to clarify the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme. India's ethanol blending targets were met through improved governance and planning. Automakers were consulted on E20 fuel compatibility and vehicle performance. Ethanol prices are fixed to ensure fair returns to farmers. The EBP Programme has saved forex and reduced carbon emissions significantly.

items.title: Savings rate vs investment returns: Why saving more matters more in the early years
items.summary: Young investors should prioritize saving more money consistently. A higher savings rate builds a stronger investment base for future growth. This approach creates a significant lead over chasing higher returns initially. Moderate returns on a larger corpus yield better long-term wealth creation. Consistent contributions and a disciplined savings habit are crucial for financial success.

items.title: Micron's mega US expansion: Is the stock poised for more upside?
items.summary: Micron has raised its planned US investment to more than $250 billion through 2035 to expand semiconductor manufacturing and meet surging AI-driven demand for advanced memory chips. The move strengthens the domestic chip supply chain, boosts investor confidence and reinforces Micron's long-term growth strategy.

items.title: Adani flagship is the next big India pick for Singapore’s Helios

The asset manager bought shares, citing Adani's improving legal standing and business ventures. Adani plans significant investments in data centers and digital expansion, leveraging its energy assets. This move positions Adani as a proxy for India's artificial intelligence ambitions. Helios also sees receding headwinds for India's broader economic outlook.

items.title: Rupee little changed as traders tread gingerly with eye on oil, dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee traded narrowly on Friday, supported by a softer dollar and stable oil prices. Asian currencies and regional stocks saw gains as Fed rate hike expectations eased. Renewed Middle East tensions kept traders cautious about directional positions in the currency. Analysts remain constructive on the rupee, citing Reserve Bank of India measures. India will report inflation data next week, with rate hikes anticipated.

items.title: Weak monsoon may weigh on rural demand, credit growth in coming months: S&P Global Ratings
items.summary: A weak monsoon poses risks to India's rural economy and farm incomes. Sectors like agriculture and microfinance face significant challenges from reduced rainfall. items.summary: This is the biggest ever single day sale for Bangalore-based Flipkart, since the time the company started in 2007.

items.title: Subramanian Swamy seeks ban on import of cement from Pakistan
items.summary: "Allowing imports of cement from Pakistan, therefore, carried with it the additional risk in that it provides an effective cover for smuggling of contraband goods and harmful weapons and ammunition concealed in cement bags which comes in rakes and trucks, in the hands of disruptionist elements," he said.

items.title: Moneycontrol Exclusive: Govt plans overhaul of tax administration for smooth GST rollout
items.summary: The government has readied plans for a major change in India’s tax administration structure, including overhauling the Central Board of Excise and Customs (CBEC) to ensure a glitch-free roll-out of a nation-wide goods and services tax (GST).

items.title: Sensex, Nifty wobbly; Hind Zinc, SBI, Force Motors most active
items.summary: Asian Paints, SBI, Tata Motors, HUL and Maruti Suzuki are top gainers while ONGC, Axis Bank, MM, Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp are major losers in the Sensex.

items.title: Uber  redBus partner with ixigo for train travellers
items.summary: ixigo, India’s leading mobile travel marketplace has announced partnerships with Uber and redBus to enable instant bookings for cabs and buses natively within ixigo trains app.

items.title: Workload in IT to come down due to AI; jobs to reduce: Genpact
items.summary: But along with that the employment growth rates have started to dip, NV 'Tiger' Tyagarajan says. The percentage addition of employees in India will not be same as past. And due to advancements, a workforce with higher skill sets is required for the IT industry, he says.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.

items.title: Up over 200% in 1 year, what's buzzing with Borosil Glass?
items.summary: Shares of Borosil Glass Works have increased threefold over the past one year as the company has posted strong revenue and EBIDTA growth over the past few quarters.

items.title: Why you should not be always chasing returns

items.title: Jhunjhunwala-partnered fund buys stake in TFCI: What's buzzing?

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
